title: Badge Component
content:
The `Badge` component is a presentational UI element used to display labels, statuses, or categories. It is built using `class-variance-authority` (cva) for flexible styling based on variants. ## Key Exports * **`Badge`**: The main React component for rendering a badge. * **`badgeVariants`**: A `cva` function that generates CSS class names based on the provided variants and props. This can be useful for custom styling outside of the `Badge` component. ## Props The `Badge` component accepts the following props: * **`variant`** (optional, `VariantProps`): Controls the visual appearance of the badge. Available options: * `default`: The primary badge style. * `secondary`: A secondary badge style. * `destructive`: A badge indicating a negative or destructive state. * `outline`: A badge with an outline and no background fill. * **`className`** (optional, `string`): Allows for additional CSS classes to be applied to the badge for custom styling. * **`...props`**: All other standard HTML `div` attributes are also supported. ## Usage Example ```jsx import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge'; function MyComponent() { return (
Default Badge Secondary Badge Destructive Badge Outline Badge Custom Styled
); } ``` This will render four badges with different visual styles, followed by a destructuve badge with an additional left margin applied via the `className` prop.
